text.title: Jira Workflow Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Jira Workflow Management methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each issue type (Story / Bug / Task / Epic) MUST have exactly one workflow; per-project workflow forks are forbidden unless an ADR exists.
rule.rationale: workflow forks break cross-project reporting and force every dashboard to special-case projects.

rule.statement: Transition rules (validators / conditions / post-functions) MUST be defined on the workflow, not in automation rules; mixing both is forbidden.
rule.rationale: split definitions cause one set of rules to silently override the other; debugging becomes guesswork.

rule.statement: Every workflow status MUST be mapped to one of Jira's status categories (To Do / In Progress / Done); un-categorised statuses are rejected.
rule.rationale: without category mapping, boards group statuses incorrectly and cycle-time metrics break.

rule.statement: Automation rules MUST be namespaced (e.g. project/<scope>/<purpose>) and version-tagged; un-namespaced rules are flagged for cleanup.
rule.rationale: un-namespaced automation rules accumulate and silently overlap; debugging takes hours.

rule.statement: JQL queries used by dashboards MUST be stored as shared filters with stable IDs; ad-hoc JQL embedded in dashboards is forbidden.
rule.rationale: embedded JQL changes silently when someone edits the dashboard; shared filters preserve audit trail.

rule.statement: Permission schemes MUST be scoped to roles (Developer / Reviewer / Admin), not individual users.
rule.rationale: per-user permissions rot when people change roles and create permission drift.
